revision.comment: 関連項目に大歳神社を追加。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 葛木御歳神社 |画像 = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]東持田269 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=葛木御歳神社}} |祭神 = [[年神|御歳神]] |神体 = 御歳山（[[神体山]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = 中鴨社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第二月曜日 |神事 = }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|御歳山（[[神体山]]）と大鳥居（左下）}}]] '''葛木御歳神社'''（かつらぎみとしじんじゃ/かつらぎみとせじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]東持田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 全国にある御歳神社・[[大歳神社]]の総本社を称する。[[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社）・[[鴨都波神社]]（下鴨社）に対して「中鴨社」と称される。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''[[年神|御歳神]]'''（みとしのかみ、三歳神/御年神） ; 相殿神 :* [[年神|大年神]]（おおとしのかみ） - 御歳神の父神。 :* 高照姫命（たかてるひめのみこと） 『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]での祭神は1座。同帳に「葛木御歳神社」と見えるように、御歳神（三歳神/御年神）を祀る神社とされる。『[[古事記]]』によると御歳神は[[須佐之男命]]の孫にあたり、大年神（スサノオの子）と[[香用比売]]（かよひめ）の間に生まれたという。「御歳」は「御稔（みとし）」の意で穀物を司る神とされ{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 5|1989年|pp=444-445}}、朝廷の[[祈年祭]]では最も重要な神として祀られていた{{Sfn|御年神（国史）}}。御歳神に関して、『[[古語拾遺]]』では御歳神の祟りで苗が枯れたので白馬・白猪・白鶏を献じるようになったという説話が見えるほか、『延喜式』祈年祭条では御歳神にやはり白馬・白猪・白鶏各1つを加える旨が定められている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 一方他文献では、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』『先代旧事本紀』「地祇本紀」。に「高照光姫大神命 坐倭国葛上郡御歳神社」の記載があるほか、『大神分身類社鈔』にも「長柄比売神社一座、大和国葛上郡、曰御歳神社、高照光姫命」と見え、高照光姫命を祭神とする説が存在する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。この高照光姫命祭神説に関して、[[本居宣長]]の『[[古事記伝]]』では、神名帳において[[鴨都波神社|鴨都波八重事代主命神社]]の次に葛木御歳神社が掲載されていたために、[[事代主]]の妹神である高照光姫命をおしあてたに過ぎないと推測する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 平林章仁は、上述の『古語拾遺』の説話には殺牛祭祀や薬用植物の知識が見えることから、古代豪族の[[葛城氏]]の配下にあった渡来系集団が御歳神の祭祀形成に影響を与えたとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。この説において、同様の殺牛祭祀の伝承が[[記紀]]の[[アメノヒボコ|天日槍]]や[[都怒我阿羅斯等]]の渡来説話にも見え、それらの説話で[[比売許曽神社]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]）の起源譚が語られることから、比売許曽神社祭神の[[シタテルヒメ|下照比売神]]（したてるひめ）が春先の殺牛農耕祭祀で「御歳神」として祀られる太陽女神であったとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。加えて下照比売神は葛城地方の神社にも見えることから、葛木御歳神社の祭神も元々は下照比売神であったと指摘される{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。 しかし、上述の『古語拾遺』の御歳神の話は蝗（いなご）の害を除くために牛を捧げる祭祀の話であり、アメノヒボコ等の説話との共通点は牛というワードが一致する程度である。また、平林は「御歳神＝タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメ」と推論するが、『古語拾遺』で御歳神はその子から「父」とされており、男性神である平林はこれを記述まちがいとする。。さらに、彼はタカテルヒメとシタテルヒメの神の名前が太陽神の神格を意味するという理由だけで二人を同一の神としているが、タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメという構図が成り立たなければ上述の彼の推論は成り立たなくなる点に注意を払う必要がある。 また、天日槍の妻である[[比売許曽命]]（息長大姫刀自命）は、三上氏の系図に[[天津彦根命]]の娘とされており、本来は下照比売神と全くの別神である[[中田憲信]]「三上氏」『諸系譜』第一冊。。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。[[神体山]]とする御歳山（みとしやま、三歳山）北麓に鎮座する。 『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）牒『新抄格勅符抄』巻10（神事諸家封戸）大同元年（806年）牒。によると、当時の「御歳神」には[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]が13戸あり、うち[[天平神護]]元年（[[765年]]）に[[大和国]]から3戸、[[讃岐国]]から10戸が充てられたという{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）に「御歳神」の[[神階]]が従二位、のち正二位に進められた旨のほか、[[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）には従一位に昇叙されたことが記されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。また貞観12年（[[870年]]）『日本三代実録』貞観12年（870年）7月22日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、河内国に堤防を築くに際し洪水を避けるため、水源の大和国の三歳神ほか[[大和神社|大和神]]・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]・[[龍田大社|竜田神]]に祈願の奉幣があった{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 神職に関しては、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』『新撰姓氏録』大和国未定雑姓 三歳祝条。において大和国内に「三歳祝」の記載があり、同氏を[[大物主|大物主神]]五世孫の意富太多根子命（[[大田田根子]]）の後裔と記載する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。ただし『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観8年（866年）条『日本三代実録』貞観8年（866年）2月13日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、三歳神には古くから神主が無かったため新たにこれを置いたが、祟りがあったため停止したとある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。この両書の記述の解釈は明らかでない{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では大和国[[葛上郡]]に「葛木御歳神社 名神大 月次新嘗」として、[[名神大社]]に列するとともに朝廷の[[月次祭]]・[[新嘗祭]]に際しては幣帛に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。なお、同帳では大和国[[高市郡]]にも「御歳神社」の記載がある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 [[平治]]元年（[[1159年]]）9月2日の「大和国目代下知状案」では「大三歳社」と見える{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。その後の変遷は不詳。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 === 神階 === * [[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）[[4月24日]]、従二位 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）4月庚申（24日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * 仁寿2年（852年）[[10月2日]]、正二位 （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）10月甲子（2日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、正二位から従一位 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「葛木御歳神」。 == 境内 == [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（右）と拝殿（左）}}]] 本殿は一間社[[春日造]]で、江戸期春日大社本殿第一殿を移築した。現在の屋根は銅板葺である。本殿前には拝殿が建てられている。 == 摂末社 == いずれも境内社として、次の神社が鎮座する。 * 天照皇大神神社 * 高皇産霊命神社 * 神皇産霊命神社 * 味鋤高彦根命神社 * 事代主命神社 * 一言主命神社 * 天稚彦命神社 * 稚日女命神社 * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == 葛木御歳神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 奥山注連縄かけ登拝 （1月第2日曜） * 祈年祭、湯立て神事 （2月第2日曜） * 崇敬会大祭 （4月第2日曜） * 御田祭り （5月3日） * 夏越大祓式 （7月最終日曜） * 秋祭り ** 宵宮 （10月第2日曜） ** 本祭り 奉納行事（10月体育の日） * 新嘗祭、奧山登拝行事 （11月23日） == アクセス == === 車 === * [[南阪奈道]]　[[葛城インターチェンジ]]から分15 * [[京奈和道]]　[[御所南インターチェンジ]]から10分 === 電車 === * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[南大阪線]][[阿倍野橋駅]]から吉野行き[[尺土駅]]で乗り換え[[御所駅]]で降り ** 徒歩約40分 ** タクシーで10分。　 ** バスは御所駅から小殿バス停下車徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 原典 === {{reflist|group="原"}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板 '''書籍''' * 地方自治体史 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=奈良県史 5 神社|publisher=名著出版|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} * 百科事典 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=|chapter=|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[原田敏明]] 「葛木御歳神社」|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[吉田敦彦]] 「御年神」|ref={{Harvid|御年神（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=葛木御歳神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * その他書籍 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=葛木御歳神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} *** [{{NDLDC|1088244/696}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）696-697コマ参照。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=池田末則|chapter=葛木御歳神社|year=1982|title=式内社調査報告 第2巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[谷川健一]]編|author=木村芳一|year=1985|chapter=葛木御歳神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 4 大和|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022143|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=平林章仁|year=2013|chapter=|title=謎の古代豪族 葛城氏（祥伝社新書326）|publisher=祥伝社|isbn=978-4396113261|ref={{Harvid|平林章仁|2013年}}}} '''サイト''' * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401|title=葛木御歳神社（大和国葛上郡）|publisher=國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」|accessdate=2015-07-30|ref={{Harvid|神道・神社史料集成}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大歳神社]] * [[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社） * [[鴨都波神社]]（下鴨社） * [[祈年祭]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ 葛木御歳神社] - 公式サイト * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html 葛木御歳神社] - 御所市観光協会 * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%E3%82%AB%E8%A1%8C/%E8%91%9B%E6%9C%A8%E5%BE%A1%E6%AD%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 葛木御歳神社] - 奈良県神社庁 * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 葛木御歳神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきみとししんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきみとししんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏|社かつらきみとししんしや]] [[Category:葛城氏|社かつらきみとししんしや]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:春日移し]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |AlternateName = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 葛木御歳神社 |画像 = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]東持田269 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=葛木御歳神社}} |祭神 = [[年神|御歳神]] |神体 = 御歳山（[[神体山]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = 中鴨社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第二月曜日 |神事 = }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|{{center|御歳山（[[神体山]]）と大鳥居（左下）}}]] '''葛木御歳神社'''（かつらぎみとしじんじゃ/かつらぎみとせじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]東持田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 全国にある御歳神社・[[大歳神社]]の総本社を称する。[[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社）・[[鴨都波神社]]（下鴨社）に対して「中鴨社」と称される。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 ; 主祭神 :* '''[[年神|御歳神]]'''（みとしのかみ、三歳神/御年神） ; 相殿神 :* [[年神|大年神]]（おおとしのかみ） - 御歳神の父神。 :* 高照姫命（たかてるひめのみこと） 『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]での祭神は1座。同帳に「葛木御歳神社」と見えるように、御歳神（三歳神/御年神）を祀る神社とされる。『[[古事記]]』によると御歳神は[[須佐之男命]]の孫にあたり、大年神（スサノオの子）と[[香用比売]]（かよひめ）の間に生まれたという。「御歳」は「御稔（みとし）」の意で穀物を司る神とされ{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 5|1989年|pp=444-445}}、朝廷の[[祈年祭]]では最も重要な神として祀られていた{{Sfn|御年神（国史）}}。御歳神に関して、『[[古語拾遺]]』では御歳神の祟りで苗が枯れたので白馬・白猪・白鶏を献じるようになったという説話が見えるほか、『延喜式』祈年祭条では御歳神にやはり白馬・白猪・白鶏各1つを加える旨が定められている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 一方他文献では、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』『先代旧事本紀』「地祇本紀」。に「高照光姫大神命 坐倭国葛上郡御歳神社」の記載があるほか、『大神分身類社鈔』にも「長柄比売神社一座、大和国葛上郡、曰御歳神社、高照光姫命」と見え、高照光姫命を祭神とする説が存在する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。この高照光姫命祭神説に関して、[[本居宣長]]の『[[古事記伝]]』では、神名帳において[[鴨都波神社|鴨都波八重事代主命神社]]の次に葛木御歳神社が掲載されていたために、[[事代主]]の妹神である高照光姫命をおしあてたに過ぎないと推測する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 平林章仁は、上述の『古語拾遺』の説話には殺牛祭祀や薬用植物の知識が見えることから、古代豪族の[[葛城氏]]の配下にあった渡来系集団が御歳神の祭祀形成に影響を与えたとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。この説において、同様の殺牛祭祀の伝承が[[記紀]]の[[アメノヒボコ|天日槍]]や[[都怒我阿羅斯等]]の渡来説話にも見え、それらの説話で[[比売許曽神社]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]）の起源譚が語られることから、比売許曽神社祭神の[[シタテルヒメ|下照比売神]]（したてるひめ）が春先の殺牛農耕祭祀で「御歳神」として祀られる太陽女神であったとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。加えて下照比売神は葛城地方の神社にも見えることから、葛木御歳神社の祭神も元々は下照比売神であったと指摘される{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。 しかし、上述の『古語拾遺』の御歳神の話は蝗（いなご）の害を除くために牛を捧げる祭祀の話であり、アメノヒボコ等の説話との共通点は牛というワードが一致する程度である。また、平林は「御歳神＝タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメ」と推論するが、『古語拾遺』で御歳神はその子から「父」とされており、男性神である平林はこれを記述まちがいとする。。さらに、彼はタカテルヒメとシタテルヒメの神の名前が太陽神の神格を意味するという理由だけで二人を同一の神としているが、タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメという構図が成り立たなければ上述の彼の推論は成り立たなくなる点に注意を払う必要がある。 また、天日槍の妻である[[比売許曽命]]（息長大姫刀自命）は、三上氏の系図に[[天津彦根命]]の娘とされており、本来は下照比売神と全くの別神である[[中田憲信]]「三上氏」『諸系譜』第一冊。。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。[[神体山]]とする御歳山（みとしやま、三歳山）北麓に鎮座する。 『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）牒『新抄格勅符抄』巻10（神事諸家封戸）大同元年（806年）牒。によると、当時の「御歳神」には[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]が13戸あり、うち[[天平神護]]元年（[[765年]]）に[[大和国]]から3戸、[[讃岐国]]から10戸が充てられたという{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）に「御歳神」の[[神階]]が従二位、のち正二位に進められた旨のほか、[[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）には従一位に昇叙されたことが記されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。また貞観12年（[[870年]]）『日本三代実録』貞観12年（870年）7月22日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、河内国に堤防を築くに際し洪水を避けるため、水源の大和国の三歳神ほか[[大和神社|大和神]]・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]・[[龍田大社|竜田神]]に祈願の奉幣があった{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 神職に関しては、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』『新撰姓氏録』大和国未定雑姓 三歳祝条。において大和国内に「三歳祝」の記載があり、同氏を[[大物主|大物主神]]五世孫の意富太多根子命（[[大田田根子]]）の後裔と記載する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。ただし『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観8年（866年）条『日本三代実録』貞観8年（866年）2月13日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、三歳神には古くから神主が無かったため新たにこれを置いたが、祟りがあったため停止したとある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。この両書の記述の解釈は明らかでない{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では大和国[[葛上郡]]に「葛木御歳神社 名神大 月次新嘗」として、[[名神大社]]に列するとともに朝廷の[[月次祭]]・[[新嘗祭]]に際しては幣帛に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。なお、同帳では大和国[[高市郡]]にも「御歳神社」の記載がある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 [[平治]]元年（[[1159年]]）9月2日の「大和国目代下知状案」では「大三歳社」と見える{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。その後の変遷は不詳。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 === 神階 === * [[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）[[4月24日]]、従二位 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）4月庚申（24日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * 仁寿2年（852年）[[10月2日]]、正二位 （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）10月甲子（2日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、正二位から従一位 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「葛木御歳神」。 == 境内 == [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（右）と拝殿（左）}}]] 本殿は一間社[[春日造]]で、江戸期春日大社本殿第一殿を移築した。現在の屋根は銅板葺である。本殿前には拝殿が建てられている。 == 摂末社 == いずれも境内社として、次の神社が鎮座する。 * 天照皇大神神社 * 高皇産霊命神社 * 神皇産霊命神社 * 味鋤高彦根命神社 * 事代主命神社 * 一言主命神社 * 天稚彦命神社 * 稚日女命神社 * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == 葛木御歳神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 奥山注連縄かけ登拝 （1月第2日曜） * 祈年祭、湯立て神事 （2月第2日曜） * 崇敬会大祭 （4月第2日曜） * 御田祭り （5月3日） * 夏越大祓式 （7月最終日曜） * 秋祭り ** 宵宮 （10月第2日曜） ** 本祭り 奉納行事（10月体育の日） * 新嘗祭、奧山登拝行事 （11月23日） == アクセス == === 車 === * [[南阪奈自動車道]] ** [[葛城インターチェンジ]]から車で15分 ** [[御所南インターチェンジ]]から車で10分 === 電車 === * '''葛木御歳神社'''（かつらぎみとしじんじゃ/かつらぎみとせじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]東持田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 全国にある御歳神社・[[大歳神社]]の総本社を称する。[[高鴨神社]]（上鴨社）・[[鴨都波神社]]（下鴨社）に対して「中鴨社」と称される。 == 祭神 == 『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]での祭神は1座。同帳に「葛木御歳神社」と見えるように、御歳神（三歳神/御年神）を祀る神社とされる。『[[古事記]]』によると御歳神は[[須佐之男命]]の孫にあたり、大年神（スサノオの子）と[[香用比売]]（かよひめ）の間に生まれたという。「御歳」は「御稔（みとし）」の意で穀物を司る神とされ{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}{{Sfn|奈良県史 坐倭国葛上郡御歳神社」の記載があるほか、『大神分身類社鈔』にも「長柄比売神社一座、大和国葛上郡、曰御歳神社、高照光姫命」と見え、高照光姫命を祭神とする説が存在する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。この高照光姫命祭神説に関して、[[本居宣長]]の『[[古事記伝]]』では、神名帳において[[鴨都波神社|鴨都波八重事代主命神社]]の次に葛木御歳神社が掲載されていたために、[[事代主]]の妹神である高照光姫命をおしあてたに過ぎないと推測する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 平林章仁は、上述の『古語拾遺』の説話には殺牛祭祀や薬用植物の知識が見えることから、古代豪族の[[葛城氏]]の配下にあった渡来系集団が御歳神の祭祀形成に影響を与えたとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。この説において、同様の殺牛祭祀の伝承が[[記紀]]の[[アメノヒボコ|天日槍]]や[[都怒我阿羅斯等]]の渡来説話にも見え、それらの説話で[[比売許曽神社]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]）の起源譚が語られることから、比売許曽神社祭神の[[シタテルヒメ|下照比売神]]（したてるひめ）が春先の殺牛農耕祭祀で「御歳神」として祀られる太陽女神であったとする{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。加えて下照比売神は葛城地方の神社にも見えることから、葛木御歳神社の祭神も元々は下照比売神であったと指摘される{{Sfn|平林章仁|2013年|pp=230-235}}。 しかし、上述の『古語拾遺』の御歳神の話は蝗（いなご）の害を除くために牛を捧げる祭祀の話であり、アメノヒボコ等の説話との共通点は牛というワードが一致する程度である。また、平林は「御歳神＝タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメ」と推論するが、『古語拾遺』で御歳神はその子から「父」とされており、男性神である平林はこれを記述まちがいとする。。さらに、彼はタカテルヒメとシタテルヒメの神の名前が太陽神の神格を意味するという理由だけで二人を同一の神としているが、タカテルヒメ＝シタテルヒメという構図が成り立たなければ上述の彼の推論は成り立たなくなる点に注意を払う必要がある。 また、天日槍の妻である[[比売許曽命]]（息長大姫刀自命）は、三上氏の系図に[[天津彦根命]]の娘とされており、本来は下照比売神と全くの別神である[[中田憲信]]「三上氏」『諸系譜』第一冊。。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。[[神体山]]とする御歳山（みとしやま、三歳山）北麓に鎮座する。 『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）牒『新抄格勅符抄』巻10（神事諸家封戸）大同元年（806年）牒。によると、当時の「御歳神」には[[神戸 (民戸)|神戸]]が13戸あり、うち[[天平神護]]元年（[[765年]]）に[[大和国]]から3戸、[[讃岐国]]から10戸が充てられたという{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）に「御歳神」の[[神階]]が従二位、のち正二位に進められた旨のほか、[[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）には従一位に昇叙されたことが記されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。また貞観12年（[[870年]]）『日本三代実録』貞観12年（870年）7月22日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、河内国に堤防を築くに際し洪水を避けるため、水源の大和国の三歳神ほか[[大和神社|大和神]]・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]・[[龍田大社|竜田神]]に祈願の奉幣があった{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 神職に関しては、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』『新撰姓氏録』大和国未定雑姓 三歳祝条。において大和国内に「三歳祝」の記載があり、同氏を[[大物主|大物主神]]五世孫の意富太多根子命（[[大田田根子]]）の後裔と記載する{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。ただし『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観8年（866年）条『日本三代実録』貞観8年（866年）2月13日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。には、三歳神には古くから神主が無かったため新たにこれを置いたが、祟りがあったため停止したとある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。この両書の記述の解釈は明らかでない{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（神々）|1985年}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では大和国[[葛上郡]]に「葛木御歳神社 名神大 月次新嘗」として、[[名神大社]]に列するとともに朝廷の[[月次祭]]・[[新嘗祭]]に際しては幣帛に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}。なお、同帳では大和国[[高市郡]]にも「御歳神社」の記載がある{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 [[平治]]元年（[[1159年]]）9月2日の「大和国目代下知状案」では「大三歳社」と見える{{Sfn|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。その後の変遷は不詳。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 === 神階 === * [[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）[[4月24日]]、従二位 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）4月庚申（24日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * 仁寿2年（852年）[[10月2日]]、正二位 （『日本文徳天皇実録』）『日本文徳天皇実録』仁寿2年（852年）10月甲子（2日）条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「御歳神」。 * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、正二位から従一位 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）『日本三代実録』貞観元年（859年）正月27日条（{{Harvnb|神道・神社史料集成}}参照）。 - 表記は「葛木御歳神」。 == 境内 == [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja shaden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（右）と拝殿（左）}}]] 本殿は一間社[[春日造]]で、江戸期春日大社本殿第一殿を移築した。現在の屋根は銅板葺である。本殿前には拝殿が建てられている。 == 摂末社 == いずれも境内社として、次の神社が鎮座する。 * 天照皇大神神社 * 高皇産霊命神社 * 神皇産霊命神社 * 味鋤高彦根命神社 * 事代主命神社 * 一言主命神社 * 天稚彦命神社 * 稚日女命神社 * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == 葛木御歳神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 奥山注連縄かけ登拝 （1月第2日曜） * 祈年祭、湯立て神事 （2月第2日曜） * 崇敬会大祭 （4月第2日曜） * 御田祭り （5月3日） * 夏越大祓式 （7月最終日曜） * 秋祭り ** 宵宮 （10月第2日曜） ** 本祭り 奉納行事（10月体育の日） * 新嘗祭、奧山登拝行事 （11月23日） == アクセス == === 車 === * [[南阪奈道路]] [[葛城インターチェンジ]]から車で15分 * [[京奈和自動車道]] [[御所南インターチェンジ]]から車で10分 === 電車 === * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄南大阪線|南大阪線]][[御所駅]] ** 徒歩約40分 ** タクシー10分 ** バス：御所駅から小殿バス停下車徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 原典 === {{reflist|group="原"}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板 '''書籍''' * 地方自治体史 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=奈良県史 5 神社|publisher=名著出版|isbn=462601335X|ref={{Harvid|奈良県史 5|1989年}}}} * 百科事典 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=|chapter=|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=[[原田敏明]] 「葛木御歳神社」|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[吉田敦彦]] 「御年神」|ref={{Harvid|御年神（国史）}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=葛木御歳神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582490305|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * その他書籍 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）696-697コマ参照。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=池田末則|chapter=葛木御歳神社|year=1982|title=式内社調査報告 第2巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|葛木御歳神社（式内社）|1982年}}}} ** book|和書|editor=|author=平林章仁|year=2013|chapter=|title=謎の古代豪族 葛城氏（祥伝社新書326）|publisher=祥伝社|isbn=978-4396113261|ref={{Harvid|平林章仁|2013年}}}} '''サイト''' * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401|title=葛木御歳神社（大和国葛上郡）|publisher=國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」|accessdate=2015-07-30|ref={{Harvid|神道・神社史料集成}}}} == 関連項目 == * {{Shrine |Name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |AlternateName = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

{{Shrine |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]

A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine-qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]]
