revision.comment: Text replacement - "|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}" to "|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}[[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}

Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]] Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}[[Category:11]] Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|25|21.42|N|135|43|26.19|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami}} (Mitoshi no Kami) |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[en:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara Prefecture|ja|御所市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=draft}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=draft}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|12=draft}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|ja|年神|1=Toshigami|12=draft}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=draft}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|ja|葛城氏|12=draft}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=draft}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1873 under the modern shrine rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=draft}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道|12=draft}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=draft}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|ja|京奈和自動車道|12=draft}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[en:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine}}
