It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''[[:en:Toshigami|Toshigami]]''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]].{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Keinawa Expressway]]: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]]: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620955}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''[[:en:Toshigami|Toshigami]]''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]].{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Keinawa Expressway]]: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]]: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620955}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''[[:en:Toshigami|Toshigami]]''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]].{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via [[:en:Keinawa Expressway|Keinawa Expressway]]: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]]: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620955}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsuragi clan]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620955]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi mitoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620955]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi mitoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG |location = [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara Prefecture]], Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4226167|N|135.7239417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October |location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''[[:en:Toshigami|Toshigami]]''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the [[:en:Katsuragi clan|Katsuragi clan]] and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]].{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]]: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620955]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi mitoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11620955]] Wikidata name: Katsuragi mitoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4226167|N|135.7239417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October |location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|en|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663|lt=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620955}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]] During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4226167|N|135.7239417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October |location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|en|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663|lt=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4226167|N|135.7239417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October |location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334}} }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|en|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663|lt=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja]][[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja haiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture|1=Gose, Nara}}, Higashi-Mochida 269 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4226167|N|135.7239417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663|1=Toshigami}} |SacredObject = Mitoshi-yama (Sacred Mountain) |Rank = {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |MainHall = One-bay Kasuga-zukuri |Alternatename = Nakakamo Shrine |Festivals = Second Monday of October |location = {{ill|Gose|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}} }} [[File:Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja otorii and Mitoshi-yama.JPG|thumb|240px|right|Sacred Mountain Mitoshi-yama and Great Torii (bottom left)]] '''Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine''' (葛木御歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara Prefecture|1=Gose, Nara}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine claims to be the head shrine of all Mitoshi and {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrines|ja|大歳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi Shrines|qq=}} nationwide. It is also referred to as the "Nakakamo Shrine" in relation to {{ill|Takakamo Shrine|ja|高鴨神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakamo Shrine|1=Takakamo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673295}} (Upper Kamo Shrine) and {{ill|Kamotsuwake Shrine|ja|鴨都波神社|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotsuwake Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tsuba Shrine|qq=}} (Lower Kamo Shrine). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following three deities:Shrine informational booklet. ; Main deity :* '''{{ill|Toshigami|en|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663|lt=Toshigami|1=Toshigami}}''' (Mitoshi no Kami, also called Sansai no Kami or Mitoshi no Kami) ; Deities in the Side Hall :* {{ill|Ōtoshi no Kami|ja|大年神|1=Toshigami|lt=Ōtoshi-no-kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神}} – Father of Mitoshi no Kami. :* Takateruhime no Mikoto (高照姫命) The deity listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} is a single enshrined deity identified as Mitoshi no Kami, associated with agriculture and grain. According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Mitoshi no Kami is a grandchild of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, born to Ōtoshi no Kami and Kayohime. Revered as a deity of harvest, Mitoshi no Kami played a crucial role in the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} rituals of the imperial court.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Kokushi)}}. Other sources, such as the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, list Takateruhime as the deity of the shrine, but this interpretation is contested.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. Modern scholars suggest that the worship of Mitoshi no Kami was influenced by interactions with ancient clans like the {{ill|Katsuragi clan|en|Katsuragi clan|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi clan|1=Katsuragi clan}} and their migratory connections.{{Harvnb|Hirabayashi, Akihito|2013}}. == History == === Origins === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is situated at the northern base of {{ill|Mitoshi-yama|ja|御歳山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoshi-yama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the sacred mountain regarded as its object of worship. === Records and Recognition === In the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, "Mitoshi no Kami" is mentioned as receiving offerings during the construction of levees in neighboring provinces to prevent flooding.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Heibonsha)}}. During the Heian period, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and received offerings during imperial rituals such as the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.{{Harvnb|Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine (Shikinaisha)}}. === Modern Era === After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1873 under the modern shrine Rank system.{{Harvnb|Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1912}}. == Facilities == The main hall, constructed in the Kasuga-zukuri style, was relocated from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and features a copper roof. A haiden (worship hall) stands in front of the main hall. == Associated Shrines == Several smaller shrines are located within the precincts, including: * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine * Takamimusubi Shrine * Kamimusubi Shrine * Ajisukitakahikone Shrine * Kotoshironushi Shrine * Hitokotonushi Shrine * Ame-wakahiko Shrine * Wakahime Shrine * Sorei Shrine == Festivals == The following annual festivals are observed: * New Year's Festival (January 1) * Sacred Rope Ritual and Climbing of the Mountain (Second Sunday of January) * Kinen-sai and Hot Water Offering (Second Sunday of February) * Devotee's Festival (Second Sunday of April) * Rice Field Festival (May 3) * Summer Purification Ceremony (Last Sunday of July) * Autumn Festival ''' Eve (Second Sunday of October) ''' Main Festival (Second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai and Mountain Climbing (November 23) == Access == === By Car === * Via {{ill|Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja|南阪奈道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Hanna Expressway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南阪奈道路|1=Minami-Hanna Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6862872}}: From {{ill|Katsuragi Interchange|ja|葛城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Interchange|1=Katsuragi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8867899}}, 15 minutes * Via {{ill|Keinawa Expressway|en|Keinawa Expressway|WD=Q11374262|lt=Keinawa Expressway|1=Keinawa Expressway}}: From {{ill|Gose Minami Interchange|ja|御所南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gose Minami Interchange|1=Gose-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071185}}, 10 minutes === By Train === * {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}: From Abenobashi Station, transfer at Shakudo Station to Gose Station ''' Walk: Approximately 40 minutes ''' Taxi: Approximately 10 minutes ''' Bus: From Gose Station to Odono Bus Stop, followed by a 10-minute walk == External Links == {{Commonscat|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} * [http://www.mitoshijinja.jp/ Official Website of Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] * [https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001400.html Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Gose City Tourism Association * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=21401 Katsuragi Mitoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University Database {{translated page|ja|葛木御歳神社|version=103392521|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:葛木御歳神社]] {{Commons category|Katsuragi-mitoshi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11620955}} [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Gose, Nara]] [[Category:History of Gose, Nara]]
